title: setup.py with suspicious imports or code execution
description: A setup.py file contains imports of os, subprocess, urllib, or socket modules which can execute arbitrary code during pip install.
remediation: Use pyproject.toml with declarative metadata instead of setup.py. Review setup.py of third-party packages before installing.

title: Python package with potential namespace confusion
description: A Python import references what appears to be a private/internal package name. If not properly scoped to a private index, an attacker can register this name on PyPI.
remediation: Configure pip to use private index for internal packages. Reserve package names on PyPI. Use --index-url and --extra-index-url correctly.

title: pip install without hash verification
description: pip install is run without --require-hashes, meaning package integrity is not verified. A compromised PyPI mirror could serve modified packages.
remediation: Use pip install --require-hashes with hash-pinned requirements. Generate hashes with pip-compile --generate-hashes.
